statute: New York General Business Law § 771; NYC Administrative Code § 20-387 through § 20-394
license_label: NY Home Improvement Contractor License / Registration Number
deposit_cap_notes: Many counties limit deposits to 1/3 of total contract price. NYC requires deposit not to exceed 1/3.

mandatory_clauses.label: Contractor license/registration disclosure
mandatory_clauses.note: NYC contractors must hold an NYC Home Improvement Contractor license. Nassau, Suffolk, Westchester, and Rockland counties also have local licensing requirements.

mandatory_clauses.label: Deposit limitation notice
mandatory_clauses.text: DEPOSIT LIMITATION: The deposit required under this contract shall not exceed one-third (1/3) of the total contract price. Deposit amount: ${{deposit_amount}}. Total contract price: ${{contract_price}}.

mandatory_clauses.label: Right to cancel notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF RIGHT TO CANCEL

You may cancel this contract within three business days of signing. To cancel, you must notify the contractor in writing at the address listed on this contract within three business days.

If you cancel, you are entitled to a refund of any deposit paid within 10 days of the contractor receiving your cancellation notice.

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE REGARDING MECHANIC'S LIENS

Under New York law (Lien Law Article 2), persons who perform labor or furnish materials for improvement of your property may be entitled to file a mechanic's lien against your property. If they file a lien and it is not discharged, you may be required to pay for the same work or materials twice. To protect yourself, obtain a written waiver of lien from each contractor, subcontractor, or material supplier before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)

penalties_for_non_compliance: License required for NYC. Fines up to $500/day for unlicensed contracting in NYC. Contract may be voidable for missing required elements.
notes: New York is largely governed at the local level for home improvement contracting. NYC has among the most stringent local requirements: HIC license required, $20,000 surety bond or trust fund enrollment required, workers' comp required. Nassau, Suffolk, Westchester, and Rockland counties also have local licensing requirements. Federal 3-day notice satisfies state cancellation requirements in NY.
